lead_profile_text: Purchase — 2 bed Leicester city centre. Owner based in Lagos, Nigeria. Developer offering competing management service — primary conversion barrier.
emotional_profile: International owner (Lagos-based); developer relationship likely prioritised by default; difficult conversion pattern — developer proximity wins against third-party management

top_objections.text: Developer offering competing management service — built-in relationship
top_objections.zac_response: Stayful projections, profitability plan, guest vetting and reporting tools presented

top_objections.text: International owner (Lagos-based) — remote decision making with less opportunity to build trust

top_questions.text: How do Stayful's projections compare to what the developer is offering?
top_questions.answer: Stayful profitability plan presented; commercial terms and special offer discussed

top_questions.text: What are owner stays and reporting tools like?
top_questions.answer: Owner stays and reporting tools covered in meeting

what_fell_flat: Developer relationship wins by default for international owner — convenience and existing trust override performance arguments
Remote decision-making by international owner reduces Stayful's ability to build in-meeting rapport

conversion_gaps.gap: Developer competing management offering — not identified pre-meeting
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Pre-qualify whether developer is offering management; if yes, prepare direct developer comparison deck before web meeting

conversion_gaps.gap: International owner — limited async trust-building before meeting
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Remote onboarding case studies; emphasise Slack communication and owner dashboard tools in follow-up

offer_framing: Commercial terms and special offer discussed at meeting — insufficient to overcome developer relationship
